bins.name: Regulatory flexibility for diverse load types
bins.desc: Eversource supports final rules that prioritize efficiency and flexibility rather than prescriptive uniformity. Load characteristics vary widely by purpose, use, location, and service needs, and rigid one-size-fits-all approaches will obstruct interconnection. Any rule must accommodate different load types, hybrid arrangements, and regional variations.
quotes: an overly prescriptive and restrictive final rule arising out of this proceeding could result in more harm than the status quo to prospective large load customers, interconnecting transmission owners, and the public generally
Eversource recommends that the focus of any final rule arising out of this proceeding focus on improving the efficiency of the large-load interconnection process without being overly prescriptive
implementing a strict, one-size-fits-all interconnection process will impede rather than facilitate the interconnection of these large loads

Given the extreme complexities and variability of the types of loads and their desired service arrangements, the Commission should also ensure that any final rule provides sufficient flexibility to address the different needs of large-load customers

Eversource appreciates that the ANOPR appears not to disturb, and should not disturb, certain existing beneficial transmission arrangements between utilities and their retail customers. These arrangements should also remain available to prospective large-load customers as they are often faster and more flexible than traditional federal interconnection processes
state processes must necessarily account for regional differences and be more responsive to local needs, including potentially the flexibility required by large loads

bins.name: Preserve retail transmission service pathways
bins.desc: Eversource opposes any changes that would eliminate state-jurisdictional retail arrangements for transmission-interconnected loads. Large load customers strongly prefer retail service, which combines state regulatory simplicity with transmission provider expertise, enables faster interconnection than federal cluster processes, and accounts for regional and local needs. Both existing and prospective retail interconnections should remain available.
quotes: Eversource appreciates that the ANOPR appears not to disturb, and should not disturb, certain existing beneficial transmission arrangements between utilities and their retail customers. These arrangements should also remain available to prospective large-load customers as they are often faster and more flexible than traditional federal interconnection processes
In Eversource's experience, prospective large loads overwhelmingly prefer the retail construct

the retail arrangement for transmission-interconnected electric service provides significant advantages to the customer as it allows the customer to function as an end-use, state-jurisdictional customer for the sale of electricity while simultaneously allowing the transmission provider to deploy its own expertise in the complex federal interconnection space
state processes must necessarily account for regional differences and be more responsive to local needs, including potentially the flexibility required by large loads

Retail processes can in some cases also result in the timelier interconnection of loads relative to federal-jurisdictional process, primarily due to the fixed nature of cluster study processes required by Order No. 2023
the Commission should preserve both existing and prospective retail interconnections at transmission voltages in order to efficiently interconnect large loads to the system

bins.name: Optional rather than mandatory cluster study participation for loads
bins.desc: Eversource opposes mandatory participation of large loads in generator cluster study processes. Generator cluster study timelines are incompatible with the urgent service dates and rapid development cycles of large load projects like electrification and data centers. Participation should remain optional, and the Commission should consider excluding loads from the cluster process entirely to preserve project viability.
quotes: While combined studies could result in enhanced efficiencies, the strict and often lengthy study timelines for generator interconnection may not be practically feasible with the tight timelines for the interconnection of large loads, which can either be subject to state-mandated service dates (such as through electrification) or whose viability is based on rapid development and construction (such as data centers)
to the extent any final rule arising out of this proceeding contemplates including qualifying loads in the Order No. 2023-mandated cluster study process, such inclusion should be optional

This will allow loads without major time pressures to reap the benefits and efficiencies of the cluster study process (such as reduced network upgrade costs) while not threatening the viability of other load projects for whom the cluster study process and timeline present insurmountable barriers to entry
the Commission should consider not including loads in that process at all due to the time pressures described above

bins.name: Limited demand for mandatory curtailment among load customers
bins.desc: Eversource reports that none of its current or prospective large load customers have expressed interest in mandatory or dispatchable curtailment arrangements. This finding suggests customer demand may not support regulatory requirements for curtailment functionality.
quotes: In Eversource's experience, none of its current or prospective large load customers have expressed any interest in mandatory/dispatchable curtailment
